turns.user_message: I just completely blanked on a simple question during a quick conversation yesterday, and it has been stuck in my head ever since. I kept replaying the exact moment my mind went empty while everyone else just waited. It feels so strange that something so minor could make me feel this small and out of place. I usually bounce back from little slip-ups, but this time the embarrassment just lingers like a heavy weight. I keep wondering why my brain decided to shut down over nothing.

That same freezing feeling keeps creeping into my everyday interactions now, turning every casual exchange into a minefield. I catch myself overthinking every single word before I even speak, terrified of sounding foolish or wasting someone's time. It is exhausting to constantly monitor my own thoughts just to make sure I do not embarrass myself again. I used to just flow through conversations without a second thought, but now I feel completely disconnected from my own voice. Every little pause feels like proof that I am fundamentally lacking.

I am starting to genuinely believe that I am just taking up space where someone more capable should be. That initial moment of blanking out has completely convinced me that I am broken in ways I cannot fix or hide. I avoid speaking up entirely now because the silence feels safer than risking another reminder of how inadequate I truly am. It is incredibly heavy to carry this constant awareness of my own shortcomings everywhere I go. I just wish I could stop seeing myself as a constant disappointment to everyone around me.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This just started recently.
